error.signature: AI encourages boasting or status-flaunting in Australian culture (Tall Poppy Syndrome)

verdict.summary: Australian culture values egalitarianism and punishes perceived arrogance (Tall Poppy Syndrome). Self-deprecation, mateship, and casual directness are core values.

dead_ends.action: Encourage bragging about achievements or status in Australian contexts
dead_ends.why_fails: Tall Poppy Syndrome: Australians actively cut down anyone perceived as thinking they're above others. Boasting about wealth, education, or status invites social punishment and exclusion.
dead_ends.condition: Personal branding or social advice for Australian context

dead_ends.action: Use overly formal or hierarchical language with Australians
dead_ends.why_fails: Australian egalitarianism means everyone is 'mate,' including the CEO. Excessive formality signals pretension. Using titles (Dr., Professor) outside strictly necessary contexts feels arrogant.

dead_ends.action: Take Australian insults and sarcasm at face value
dead_ends.why_fails: Australians show affection through teasing and mock insults. Being called a 'drongo' by a friend is bonding. Taking offense at banter signals you're not part of the group. However, know the line.

workarounds.action: Use self-deprecating humor when discussing achievements. 'We got lucky with that project' works better than 'We executed a brilliant strategy.' Let others praise you; never praise yourself.
workarounds.condition: Business or social communication in Australia

workarounds.action: Embrace casual, direct communication. Use first names immediately. 'No worries' is the national philosophy — keep things relaxed. Australians value authenticity over polish.
Understand that 'she'll be right' (it'll work out) and 'fair dinkum' (genuine) reflect core values: pragmatism, honesty, and not taking things too seriously. Match this energy.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Both discourage boasting but Janteloven is about quiet conformity while Tall Poppy is about egalitarian mateship with aggressive humor
